name: Underscores
description:
'''April Harper Grey''' (born April 21, 2000), known professionally as '''Underscores''' (stylized in all-lowercase), is an American singer-songwriter and producer. Born in [[San Francisco]], California, Grey began releasing [[dubstep music]] on [[SoundCloud]] at the age of 13, eventually incorporating other genres. After releasing three [[EP]]s from 2018 to 2020, she rose to prominence with her debut album [[Fishmonger (album)|''Fishmonger'']] in March 2021. She released [[Boneyard AKA Fearmonger|its companion EP]] and [[Opening act|opened]] for [[100 Gecs]] on tour later that year. Grey performed her first headlining tour in early 2022, and released her second album ''[[Wallsocket]]'' in September 2023 on the [[Mom + Pop]] record label. It was promoted with four singles, an [[alternate reality game]], and a tour of North America and Europe. In March 2026, she released her third studio album, [[U (Underscores album)|''U'']]. Grey will open on tour for [[Charli XCX]] in the fall of 2026.

Grey's early influences include [[Skrillex]] and [[100 Gecs]]. Her album ''Wallsocket'' was inspired by artists such as [[Madonna]], [[Imogen Heap]], [[Bruce Springsteen]], and [[Sufjan Stevens]], as well as country music, 2010s pop, and horror films. Her style has encompassed genres such as [[hyperpop]], [[pop-punk]], [[dubstep]], [[indie pop]], [[rock music|rock]], and [[Midwest emo]].
